headline: Oregon survey documents 467 drone airframes grounded across 25 states with national exposure between $50M and $2B as NASAO compliance deadline enforcement accelerates.

lead_story.title: NASAO Enforcement Creates Multi-State Asset Stranding Crisis
lead_story.body: Oregon Department of Transportation's February 2026 white paper revision 2 documents 467 drone airframes grounded or restricted across 23 states based on survey of 25 state DOTs, creating national exposure estimated between $50 million and $2 billion. The groundings are tied to NASAO regulatory compliance deadlines now being actively enforced at state level. This represents the largest documented mass asset stranding event in the Blue UAS transition to date, with state-level enforcement moving faster than federal timelines in multiple jurisdictions.
lead_story.action: Conduct immediate asset inventory audit against NASAO compliance requirements by state of operation, prioritize redeployment of at-risk airframes to non-restricted states or missions before Q3 2026, and accelerate replacement procurement timelines by 90-120 days to avoid capability gaps.

supply_chain.signal: 96% concentration risk — 463 of 484 tracked propeller models are Chinese-manufactured, dominated by HQProp, Gemfan, and DAL (all Shenzhen/Guangdong). Propellers currently not classified as NDAA-covered components.
supply_chain.window: Immediate vulnerability if regulatory interpretation changes or if geopolitical supply disruption occurs; no domestic alternative at scale exists.
supply_chain.action: Stockpile 18-24 months of propeller inventory for critical platforms now while supply is available; initiate dual-source qualification with any available non-PRC manufacturers even at higher unit cost; flag this exposure in all program risk registers.

supply_chain.signal: 97% concentration risk with critical compliance gap — 187 of 193 tracked FPV cameras are Chinese-manufactured, and FPV cameras ARE classified as NDAA critical components (imaging hardware with data storage/transmission capability).
supply_chain.window: 60-90 days before current inventory creates compliance violations on new procurements; existing inventory may face retroactive restrictions.
supply_chain.action: Immediately halt new procurements of PRC-manufactured FPV cameras; identify the 6 non-PRC camera models in Forge database for qualification testing; budget 3-5x unit cost premium for compliant alternatives.

sources.name: DroneClear Forge parts-db FPV camera analysis

supply_chain.signal: 70% concentration risk — 212 of 303 tracked motors are Chinese-manufactured, with T-Motor/Tiger Motors representing 62 models from Nan'an City, Fujian, plus iFlight and BrotherHobby concentrations.
supply_chain.window: 6-12 months before supply constraints impact production if export controls tighten; longer lead times than propellers but higher integration complexity.
supply_chain.action: Begin qualification testing of the 91 non-PRC motors in database for mission-critical platforms; negotiate long-term supply agreements with non-PRC manufacturers now before capacity is exhausted; plan for 15-20% performance tradeoffs.

supply_chain.signal: Confirmed diversion to adversary weapons systems — 40,000+ Raspberry Pi 4B units verified in Geran-2/3 Shahed-136 drones by Ukrainian military intelligence, core component of 'Borscht Tracker V3' navigation system.
supply_chain.window: Export control tightening likely within 90 days; secondary market prices already volatile; procurement scrutiny increasing.
supply_chain.action: Document end-use cases for all RPi purchases with enhanced due diligence; shift to alternative single-board computers where possible (Nvidia Jetson, Intel NUC variants); expect 30-45 day delays on new RPi orders due to export licensing.

gray_zone.status: Gray zone score 0.782 (CRITICAL) — Texas DTPA lawsuit filed February 18, 2026 in Collin County with 7 counts, no response filed after 45+ days, case frozen pending injunctive relief.
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise with matching board part numbers, and controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI hardware. Firmware is signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys with DJI retaining root key control, built on DJI SDK. Product discontinuation confirmed due to DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement active Q1-Q2 2026, creating adversary component shortage. PIE ensemble model predicts 91% probability of full product line discontinuation in Q2 2026.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Any agency with Anzu Raptor acquisitions faces stranded asset risk within 60-90 days as parts supply chain collapses and firmware update capability terminates; Texas state agencies face immediate legal compliance exposure pending injunction outcome.
gray_zone.action: Freeze all new Anzu purchases immediately; migrate mission-critical operations off Anzu platforms before Q3 2026; file warranty claims now while company is still operational; prepare full asset write-off in FY2026-2027 budget cycles.

gray_zone.status: Gray zone score 0.850 (CRITICAL) — highest-risk gray zone entity tracked, subject to FCC Covered List with S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) inventory still available at retail despite prohibition on new model authorizations.
gray_zone.development: Firmware analysis by Jon Sawyer confirms SkyRover app connects DIRECTLY to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys, and OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company structure identified: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. FCC Covered List status prohibits new authorizations but existing inventory remains in distribution channel creating buyer compliance risk.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Purchasers of existing S1/X1 inventory face firmware update prohibition starting January 2027 per FCC timeline, creating stranded assets; any agency purchase after FCC Covered List date (December 22, 2025) may face retroactive compliance violations.
gray_zone.action: Issue procurement ban immediately on all SkyRover products regardless of retail availability; audit any purchases made between December 2025 and present for compliance review; do not rely on existing FCC authorizations as retroactive revocation authority is active.

watch_list.item: FLIR Boson 640 thermal camera supply tightening
watch_list.why: 10 of 43 tracked thermal cameras use FLIR Boson 640 (23% concentration), with AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp driving demand competition.
watch_list.trigger: Lead times exceeding 26 weeks or allocation notices from Teledyne FLIR would force immediate redesigns on platforms in development.

watch_list.why: 87% probability prediction of stranded asset crisis as FCC Covered List entities lose OTA update capability, creating security and operational degradation across installed base.
watch_list.trigger: FCC release of specific enforcement guidance or first agency compliance letter would require immediate fleet-wide OTA capability audit.

watch_list.why: 89% probability that reported $93.3B signal is double-count artifact (two flags with 99.9% identical parameters, only $100M variance), potentially overstating market size.
watch_list.trigger: Source document review confirming double-count would require revision of multi-year procurement forecast models and budget justifications.

predictions.event: Anzu Robotics product discontinuation due to adversary component shortage
predictions.hedge: Establish alternative vendor qualifications for any missions currently dependent on Anzu platforms by May 31, 2026; redirect FY2026 Q3/Q4 Anzu purchase orders to Blue UAS List manufacturers; budget 40-60% cost premium for replacement platforms.

predictions.event: FCC Covered List firmware update prohibition creates stranded asset crisis
predictions.hedge: Conduct firmware update dependency audit across all drone assets by June 2026; prioritize replacement of any platforms requiring OTA updates for safety/security compliance; negotiate extended support contracts with non-Covered List manufacturers before capacity is exhausted.

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 thermal camera allocation shortages impact platform production
predictions.hedge: Place non-cancellable Boson 640 orders now for 18-month forward coverage; qualify alternative thermal cores (BAE, Lynred) on non-critical platforms immediately; accept 10-15% performance degradation to maintain production schedules.

signal_summary.top_concern: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M-$2B exposure
